question: Why did Hurricane-Beryl claim severity for tier-2 commercial wind exposure exceed our model by 38% in coastal Texas and Louisiana?
metric_label: CAT wind severity by tier, region, and day

upstream_models.description: Paid and open claims from the SQL Server Claims Management system via Fivetran CDC. Includes loss_date, peril_code, policy_id, incurred_loss, and paid_loss.
Policy master from Oracle PAS. Includes tier classification (tier-1 through tier-4), coverage_type, state_code, county_fips, debris_removal_sublimit, and effective dates.
Annual written premium by policy. Includes exposure_basis, rate_change_pct, and cat_load applied at last renewal. Used to compute loss ratio and model adequacy.
Peril master including storm classification, NOAA event linkage, and regional model assumption set. Hurricane-Beryl maps to peril_code HU-2024-BERYL with Gulf Coast zone flags.
